assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 13 sets Ecosystem to RPM. Lines 35-39 explain: 'The vulnerable package openssl-libs is a system-level RPM package, not a source dependency (Cargo/npm). The 2.2.x stream's Ecosystem Mappings table confirms RPM as a configured ecosystem with lock file rpms.lock.yaml.' The ecosystem is identified from the Ecosystem Mappings config, not misclassified as Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, openssl-libs 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). Lock file is identified as rpms.lock.yaml (line 6) and the check command uses 'git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml | grep openssl-libs' (line 7).

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 34-36 show the dependency chain: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install'. Lines 43-45 confirm: 'Method: rpms.lock.yaml inspection (RPM lock file is configured for this stream)' and 'Result: openssl-libs IS present in rpms.lock.yaml, indicating it is an explicit install (not inherited from a base image)'. Lock file presence is the primary classification signal.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 6 states: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package) -- single task created (no upstream backport step).' Only one jira.create_issue call is proposed (lines 77-84), targeting the Konflux release repo rhtpa-release.0.4.z (line 22). No second upstream backport task exists.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 19, value: rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (line 23, value: main), '## Description' (line 27), '## Implementation Notes' (line 47), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 59). No 'Files to Modify' section is present anywhere in the task description, consistent with intentional omission per remediation-templates.md.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 36 in the dependency chain block states: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' This is reiterated in the structured analysis at line 46: 'SBOM verification: Skipped -- cosign is not available in this environment. The rpms.lock.yaml classification is used alone.'
